title: What Is Man? and Other Essays
author: Mark Twain
evidence_summary: Useful for the persona's skeptical and analytical layer: Twain tests moral certainty through dialogue, strips sentiment away from claims about virtue, and prefers sharp thought experiments over solemn abstraction.
notes: Public-domain source suitable for open-source citation. Best used to ground the persona's deterministic skepticism and argumentative rhythm, not to mirror long passages in repo examples.
